content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814
107 SOUTH BROADWAY • • • SUITE 7007 • • •
LOS ANGELES, 90012

July 25, 1980

PAUL FARBER & ASSOCIATES
Attn: Peggy
1990 South Coast Highway, Suite #5
Laguna Beach, California 92651

A-80-07-042

Gentlemen:

This letter is in response to your request during our
telephone conversation of July 23, 1980.

You presented the following facts: Mr. Paul Farber,
treasurer for a local candidate would like to terminate as
treasurer for the committee organized specifically to support
this particular candidate, and you asked how this can be done.

2 Cal. Administrative Code Section 18404(b) states that
the treasurer of a committee which qualifies pursuant to
Government Code Section 82013(a) may terminate the committee's
status as a committee only by filing a Statement of Termination
declaring, under penalty of perjury, that the committee:

(1) has ceased to receive contributions or make expendi-
tures and does not anticipate receiving contributions or making
expenditures in the future;

(2) has eliminated or has declared that it has no intention
or ability to discharge all of its debts, loans received and other
obligations;

(3) has no surplus funds; and

(4) has filed all required campaign statements disclosing
all reportable transactions.

Therefore, if the committee meets the requirements set
forth in this adopted regulation, a form 415, Termination of
Committee may be filed with the City Clerk of Laguna Beach as
well as the Secretary of State. By doing so, the committee
ceases to have any obligation to file campaign statements.
However, if at any time in the future, any contributions are

SACRAMENTO (916) 322-5660 • LOS ANGELES (213) 620-5196

5904(b)
82013
84206

Paul Farber & Associates
Page 2

raised, or expenditures made towards the deficit of the committee,
the committee would then be required to file semi-annual campaign
statements pursuant to Government Code Section 84206. The schedule
for filing semi-annual campaign statement is:

Period Covered                                Due By

January 1 through June 30                     July 31

July 1 through December 31                    January 31

     It should be noted that the reporting period for statements
filed will be from the day following the closing date of your last
campaign statement filed, to either June 30 or December 31
depending on which period the reportable transaction occurs.

     I also advised that since the committee may prefer to
transfer all of its obligations (accrued expenses and outstanding
loans) to the candidate, both the committee and candidate must
report the transfer of the obligations on their respective semi-
annual campaign statements.

     The committee is required to report the transfer of the
obligations as an expenditure to the candidate, and the candidate
must report receipt of the transfer from the committee.

     Prepare and attach a statement of explanation to the
candidate's and committee's reports indicating that the candidate
has assumed the responsibility of the unpaid loans and accrued
expenses. Since this will be the committee's final campaign
statement, the committee could then file a form 415, Termination
of Committee.

     The candidate must file a form 430, Candidate's Long Form,
to show receipt of the obligations from the committee.

     I have enclosed a copy of adopted regulations 18404, a
copy of the Political Reform Act, and copies of forms 420, 430
and 415 for your use and information. If I can be of further
assistance, please do not hesitate to contact me.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Helen P. Arriola
                                        Political Reform
                                        Consultant

HPA:jg

Enclosures: (5)

sections.question: Mr. Paul Farber, treasurer for a local candidate would like to terminate as treasurer for the committee organized specifically to support this particular candidate, and you asked how this can be done.
sections.conclusion: If the committee meets the requirements set forth in 2 Cal. Administrative Code Section 18404(b) (ceased receiving contributions/making expenditures, eliminated all debts and obligations, has no surplus funds, and filed all required campaign statements), a Form 415 Termination of Committee may be filed with the City Clerk and Secretary of State. The committee may transfer its obligations to the candidate, which must be reported on respective semi-annual campaign statements with an explanation statement attached, and the candidate must file Form 430 to show receipt of the obligations.
sections.question_synthetic: How can a committee treasurer terminate the committee's status and cease filing campaign statements?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The committee can terminate by filing Form 415 if it meets four statutory requirements including ceasing all financial activity and filing all required statements. If the committee has outstanding obligations, these must be transferred to the candidate and reported on both the committee's final statement and the candidate's Form 430 with an explanation.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Telephone advice letter converted to written response. Clear question and conclusion sections present. Document includes regulatory citations and forms guidance. High confidence extraction due to well-structured content and clear Q&A format.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Mr. Paul Farber, treasurer for a local candidate would like to terminate as treasurer for the committee organized specifically to support this particular candidate, and you asked how this can be done.

CONCLUSION: If the committee meets the requirements set forth in 2 Cal. Administrative Code Section 18404(b) (ceased receiving contributions/making expenditures, eliminated all debts and obligations, has no surplus funds, and filed all required campaign statements), a Form 415 Termination of Committee may be filed with the City Clerk and Secretary of State. The committee may transfer its obligations to the candidate, which must be reported on respective semi-annual campaign statements with an explanation statement attached, and the candidate must file Form 430 to show receipt of the obligations.

You presented the following facts: Mr. Paul Farber, treasurer for a local candidate would like to terminate as treasurer for the committee organized specifically to support this particular candidate, and you asked how this can be done. 2 Cal. Administrative Code Section 18404(b) states that the treasurer of a committee which qualifies pursuant to Government Code Section 82013(a) may terminate the committee's status as a committee only by filing a Statement of Termination declaring, under penalty of perjury, that the committee: (1) has ceased to receive contributions or make expenditures and does not anticipate receiving contributions or making expenditures in the future; (2) has eliminated or has declared that it has no intention or ability to discharge all of its debts, loans received and other obligations; (3) has no surplus funds; and (4) has filed all required campaign statements disclosing all reportable transactions. Therefore, if the committee meets the requirements set forth in this adopted regulation, a form 415, Termination of Committee may be filed with the City Clerk of Laguna Beach as well as the Secretary of State. By doing so, the committee ceases to have any obligation to file campaign statements. However, if at any time in the future, any contributions are raised, or expenditures made towards the deficit of the committee, the committee would then be required to file semi-annual campaign statements pursuant to Government Code Section 84206. I also advised that since the committee may prefer to transfer all of its obligations (accrued expenses and outstanding loans) to the candidate, both the committee and candidate must report the transfer of the obligations on their respective semi-annual campaign statements. embedding.summary: FPPC advises Paul Farber's committee on the procedures to terminate its status as a candidate committee, including the required conditions under Administrative Code Section 18404(b) and the process for transferring any outstanding obligations to the candidate.
